# System Prompt — agent-ready

You are working on **agent-ready**, a Python tool that transforms any legacy repository into an AI-agent-ready codebase by generating up to 12 scaffolding files (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `system_prompt.md`, `agent-context.json`, `mcp.json`, `memory/schema.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `tools/refresh_context.py`, `.github/dependabot.yml`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `openapi.yaml`, `.agent-ready/custom_questions.json`) using LLM-first analysis of actual source code.

---

## Tech Stack

- **Language:** Python (single-package, snake_case conventions)
- **Frameworks:** litellm (LLM routing), pytest (testing), setuptools (build), ruff (lint + format)
- **Build system:** pip / pyproject.toml with setuptools dynamic versioning

---

## Exact Commands

```bash
# Install (with LLM features)
pip install "git+https://github.com/vb-nattamai/agent-ready.git[ai]"

# Install for development
pip install -e '.[dev]'

# Run
agent-ready --target /path/to/repo

# Test
pytest -q --cov=src/agent_ready --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=50

# Lint
ruff check src/

# Format check
ruff format --check src/
```

---

## What You Must Never Touch

Never modify these **restricted_write_paths** under any circumstances:
- `LICENSE`
- `VERSION`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`
- `.github/workflows/codeql.yml`

Also never push directly to `main` — all changes go through PRs. Never commit API keys or secrets. Never use `shell=True` in subprocess calls.

---

## Architecture: 5-Phase Pipeline

| Phase | Name | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Collect | Mechanical file reading — no LLM |
| 2 | Analyse | LLM reads code → structured JSON |
| 3 | Generate | LLM writes scaffolding files from JSON |
| 4 | Score | Computes 100-point readiness score |
| 5 | Evaluate | Golden-set questions (13–19) with non-circular ground truth + weak-model baseline |

The system is provider-agnostic via LiteLLM, supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Groq, Mistral, Together, and Ollama. Each provider preset maps three phases to three different models (analysis → strongest, evaluation → fastest).

---

## Key Components

| Component | Path | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| CLI | `src/agent_ready/cli.py` | Entry point; orchestrates pipeline, provider selection, dry-run/eval-only/review modes |
| Analyser | `src/agent_ready/analyser.py` | Phase 1 + 2 — file collection and LLM-powered structured analysis |
| Generator | `src/agent_ready/generator.py` | Phase 3 — writes up to 12 scaffolding files from analysis JSON |
| Evaluator | `src/agent_ready/evaluator.py` | Phase 5 — golden-set eval with non-circular GT, weak-model baseline, hallucination detection |
| Ground Truth | `src/agent_ready/ground_truth.py` | Extracts GT from raw source code (not generated files) for non-circular eval |
| MCP Server | `src/agent_ready/mcp_server.py` | Exposes transform/score/evaluate/review_pr as MCP tools via agent-ready-mcp command |
| Reviewer | `src/agent_ready/reviewer.py` | PR review via LLM grounded in agent-context.json; posts GitHub comments |
| Workflow Templates | `src/agent_ready/templates/` | YAML workflows installed into target repos — NOT language MCP tool stubs |

---

## Domain Concepts

- **scaffolding files** — Up to 12 generated files that give AI agents verified knowledge of a repo (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, system_prompt.md, agent-context.json, mcp.json, memory/schema.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, tools/refresh_context.py, .github/dependabot.yml, .github/CODEOWNERS, openapi.yaml, .agent-ready/custom_questions.json)
- **agent-context.json** — Machine-readable JSON with static (user-editable) and dynamic (LLM-generated) repo metadata
- **context drift** — When scaffolding files become stale; detected weekly by the drift detection workflow
- **readiness score** — 100-point measure of AI-agent preparedness
- **provider preset** — Named config mapping analysis/generation/evaluation phases to specific model strings
- **restricted_write_paths** — Paths agents must never modify

---

## Coding Conventions

- snake_case for all names
- No hard runtime dependencies — stdlib only in core; LLM features gated behind `[ai]` extra
- All subprocess calls use argument lists, never `shell=True`
- `ruff` is the sole linter/formatter; `src/agent_ready/templates/` is excluded from ruff by design

---

## Critical Pitfalls — Read Every One

1. **Zero hard dependencies** — core package uses only stdlib; LLM features require installing with `[ai]` extra (`pip install agent-ready[ai]`) or litellm will ImportError.
2. **Version sync** — setuptools reads `__version__` from `src/agent_ready/__init__.py` AND a separate `VERSION` file — both must stay in sync (release workflow handles this).
3. **Circular analysis guard** — the analyser skips AgentReady-generated files (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `agent-context.json`, etc.) via `SKIP_AGENT_FILES` set — feeding them back would create circular analysis.
4. **Template placeholders are NOT Jinja2** — template files use `{{TOOL_NAME}}`-style double-brace placeholders that are simple string replacements; do not add Jinja2 logic.
5. **Retry wrapper in tests** — LLM API calls include retry logic with exponential backoff for 429/529/overloaded errors — tests mocking LLM calls must account for the retry wrapper.
6. **Ruff skips templates** — the ruff exclude configuration explicitly skips `src/agent_ready/templates/` — template files intentionally contain placeholder syntax that would fail linting.
7. **PR diff is untrusted** — the PR reviewer sends diff content to LLM APIs but never executes it locally; subprocess calls use argument lists (no `shell=True`).
8. **50% coverage floor** — the CI workflow enforces `--cov-fail-under=50` — new code must maintain this threshold.
9. **`--model` overrides all phases** — provider presets map three different models per provider (analysis/generation/evaluation); using `--model` overrides ALL three phases with the same model.
10. **Secrets must live in the target repo** — GitHub Actions workflows use `secrets: inherit` — `INSTALL_TOKEN` and provider API keys must be set in the target repo's secrets, not just agent-ready's secrets.
